questions.question: What is electric current?
questions.correct_answer: The rate of flow of electric charge through a conductor
questions.explanation: Electric current flows when electric charges move through a closed circuit.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: One ampere is defined as the flow of one coulomb of charge per second.

questions.question: What is potential difference?
questions.correct_answer: Work done to move a unit charge from one point to another
questions.explanation: Potential difference provides the energy needed to move electric charges in a circuit.

questions.question: What does Ohm’s law state?
questions.correct_answer: Current is directly proportional to potential difference when temperature is constant
questions.explanation: This relationship between voltage, current, and resistance is expressed as V = IR.

questions.question: What is resistance?
questions.correct_answer: Opposition offered by a conductor to the flow of electric current
questions.explanation: Resistance depends on the length, thickness, and material of the conductor.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of resistance?
questions.explanation: A conductor has resistance of one ohm if one volt produces a current of one ampere.

questions.question: How are resistors connected in series?
questions.correct_answer: They are connected end-to-end so the same current flows through each resistor
questions.explanation: The total resistance in a series circuit is equal to the sum of all individual resistances.

questions.question: How are resistors connected in parallel?
questions.correct_answer: They are connected across the same two points in a circuit
questions.explanation: Each resistor experiences the same potential difference in a parallel circuit.

questions.question: What is electric power?
questions.correct_answer: The rate at which electrical energy is consumed or converted
questions.explanation: Electric power indicates how quickly an appliance uses electrical energy.

questions.question: What is the commercial unit of electrical energy?
questions.explanation: Electricity consumption in homes is measured in kilowatt-hours, commonly called units.

notes.title: Electric Current
notes.content: The rate of flow of electric charge through a conductor in a closed circuit.

notes.title: Potential Difference
notes.content: The work done to move a unit charge between two points in a circuit.

notes.title: Ohm’s Law
notes.content: The current through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference across it when temperature remains constant.

notes.title: Resistance
notes.content: Opposition offered by a conductor to the flow of electric current.

notes.title: Electric Power
notes.content: Rate at which electrical energy is consumed in a circuit; measured in watts.

facts.fact: Electric current flows only when a circuit is closed.
The SI unit of current is ampere and the SI unit of resistance is ohm.
In series circuits the total resistance increases.
In parallel circuits the potential difference across each resistor is the same.
Electricity consumption in households is measured in kilowatt-hours.
